status: first-pass evidence bundle exported from tracker rows

observations.observation_value: Zafirlukast was reported to reduce capsular contracture in a preliminary breast implant clinical report.

author_claims.claim_text: Leukotriene receptor antagonism is a recurring host-conditioning strategy for breast implant capsular contracture prevention or treatment.
Pirfenidone provides anti-fibrotic capsule evidence in mammary implant models and clinical capsular contracture treatment.
Tranilast is represented as a prophylactic capsule-modifying comparator in implant models.
Tamoxifen can reduce silicone implant capsule formation or capsular contracture outcomes in mouse/murine models.
Breast implant capsule evidence should distinguish prophylaxis, recurrence prevention, and treatment of established contracture.
Flap preconditioning evidence supports the idea that recipient/systemic state can be modified before tissue transfer.
Systemic hypoxia-pathway preconditioning can mobilize host bone marrow-derived mechanisms and reduce skin flap necrosis.
Flap preconditioning should not be overread as anti-fibrotic conditioning unless remodeling endpoints are measured.
Alpha-1 antitrypsin and related peri-transplant anti-inflammatory strategies can target early islet engraftment barriers, but human follow-up evidence is mixed.
IBMIR/complement/coagulation-adjacent pathways are early host-response targets in islet transplantation.
Macrophage and chemokine recruitment pathways shape islet graft survival and microenvironment activation.
Regulatory immune conditioning through Tregs and tolerogenic dendritic-cell programs can improve islet engraftment or survival in preclinical settings.
Islet evidence is primarily engraftment/survival evidence and should not be treated as direct anti-fibrotic reconstruction evidence.
Organ-tolerance studies can inform host immune conditioning but are often rejection-centered rather than remodeling-centered.
Leukotriene pathway inhibition after SCI can reduce secondary injury-associated processes and improve functional endpoints in animal models.
LTB4-BLT1 signaling links leukotriene biology to neutrophil infiltration and secondary SCI injury.
Pro-resolving lipid mediator strategies can improve inflammatory resolution and recovery-associated endpoints after SCI, but co-therapy rows need attribution boundaries.
SCI leukotriene/resolution evidence should be treated as lesion-bed conditioning analogy rather than direct preoperative reconstruction evidence.
Tranilast scar studies support matrix-maturation biology but are mainly local/topical route evidence in this corpus.
Module 7 synthesis must preserve whether the intervention targets the recipient host, graft/donor, scaffold/local depot, or transplanted cells.
Route is a first-class variable because oral/systemic, transdermal, remote ischemic, biologic, local depot, and co-therapy interventions imply different host-conditioning strength.
Timing is a first-class variable because prophylaxis, perioperative conditioning, post-injury treatment, chronic treatment, and recurrence prevention are not interchangeable.
Remodeling endpoints must be kept separate from survival, rejection, function, and engraftment endpoints.
Most Module 7B rows are abstract/source-page level and should remain confidence-limited until full text confirms dose, route, timing, and endpoint details.
Cross-field evidence is hypothesis-generating for SCI reconstruction unless direct SCI reconstructive conditioning studies are available.
Review/meta-analysis rows support the existence of an implant prophylaxis literature but should not replace extraction from primary intervention studies.
Neutral or negative islet autotransplant follow-up data should be retained as a contradiction to simple anti-inflammatory benefit claims.
Human reconstructive preconditioning evidence exists but is thin in this first-pass corpus.
Recurrence-prevention studies are closest to perioperative reconstruction conditioning but still may involve patients with prior capsule biology.
Early SCI anti-inflammatory or pro-resolving treatment may be relevant to future graft-bed preparation hypotheses.
Biomaterial-delivered tolerogenic microparticles should be classified as host-directed immunomodulation only when the recipient immune response is the target endpoint.
Local scar studies are useful for Phase 4 matrix maturation but should be excluded from systemic-host-conditioning consensus claims.

Leukotriene antagonist implant evidence is triangulated across clinical reports, recurrence-prevention studies, and reviews, but agent, dose, indication, and evidence-quality boundaries remain important.
author_claims.notes: Keeps LTRA claim strong but bounded.

author_claims.claim_text: Flap preconditioning includes a mechanistic host-cell mobilization signal and at least one human reconstruction trial, but the corpus remains thin for remodeling-specific endpoints.

Human islet autotransplant studies show that peri-transplant anti-inflammatory conditioning can be tested clinically, but early engraftment rationale may not translate into durable diabetes-outcome benefit.

Module 7 evidence should be graded by directness for SCI reconstruction: direct reconstructive/implant conditioning, transplant engraftment conditioning, SCI lesion-bed analogs, and local scar biology are different evidence tiers.

boundary_records.boundary_statement: Host conditioning is distinct from donor organ conditioning, ex vivo graft/cell preconditioning, and scaffold-local drug delivery.
boundary_records.handling_in_consensus: Consensus must specify target and route.

boundary_records.boundary_statement: Breast implant capsular contracture is the strongest direct precedent, but implant capsule biology should not be directly equated with SCI graft-bed biology.
boundary_records.handling_in_consensus: Translate as precedent, not as SCI proof.

boundary_records.boundary_statement: Flap preconditioning evidence is mostly vascular/ischemia-reperfusion evidence rather than explicit anti-fibrotic remodeling evidence.
Islet transplantation evidence often measures engraftment, IBMIR, macrophage activation, and survival rather than fibrosis or tissue architecture.
Organ transplantation rows are often rejection-centered and should not be included in remodeling consensus unless remodeling/integration outcomes are explicit.
SCI leukotriene/resolution rows are lesion-bed conditioning analogs rather than direct preoperative reconstructive-surgery evidence.
boundary_records.handling_in_consensus: Use as SCI plausibility evidence only.

boundary_records.boundary_statement: Local scar or local-depot rows inform matrix biology but should not support systemic host-conditioning statements.
Perioperative timing categories are non-equivalent: prophylaxis, perioperative treatment, post-injury treatment, chronic treatment, and recurrence prevention must remain distinct.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Breast implant capsular contracture provides the strongest direct precedent that recipient-directed pharmacologic conditioning can alter fibrotic capsule outcomes, especially through leukotriene antagonists and anti-fibrotic or anti-estrogen strategies.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Breast implant capsular contracture provides the strongest direct precedent that recipient-directed pharmacologic conditioning can alter fibrotic capsule outcomes, especially through leukotriene antagonists and anti-fibrotic or anti-estrogen strategies.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Flap and ischemic-tissue studies show that recipient or remote systemic preconditioning can improve vascular survival biology before tissue transfer, but this is not yet equivalent to anti-fibrotic recipient-bed conditioning.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Flap and ischemic-tissue studies show that recipient or remote systemic preconditioning can improve vascular survival biology before tissue transfer, but this is not yet equivalent to anti-fibrotic recipient-bed conditioning.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Islet transplantation supports peri-transplant host conditioning as a strategy to reduce early inflammatory engraftment barriers, including IBMIR, complement/coagulation signaling, macrophage activation, chemokine recruitment, and regulatory immune balance.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Islet transplantation supports peri-transplant host conditioning as a strategy to reduce early inflammatory engraftment barriers, including IBMIR, complement/coagulation signaling, macrophage activation, chemokine recruitment, and regulatory immune balance.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Organ tolerance studies are useful for the host-conditioning concept but should remain boundary evidence unless rejection control is linked to remodeling, vascular integration, fibrosis, or functional graft integration.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Organ tolerance studies are useful for the host-conditioning concept but should remain boundary evidence unless rejection control is linked to remodeling, vascular integration, fibrosis, or functional graft integration.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: SCI leukotriene and pro-resolving mediator studies support the plausibility that early fibroinflammatory lesion-bed state can be modified, but they are analogs rather than direct preoperative reconstruction studies.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: SCI leukotriene and pro-resolving mediator studies support the plausibility that early fibroinflammatory lesion-bed state can be modified, but they are analogs rather than direct preoperative reconstruction studies.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Tranilast scar and hypertrophic-scar studies inform matrix-maturation biology, but local or topical delivery rows should not be used as evidence for systemic host conditioning.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Tranilast scar and hypertrophic-scar studies inform matrix-maturation biology, but local or topical delivery rows should not be used as evidence for systemic host conditioning.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Module 7 host-conditioning synthesis requires an explicit ontology for intervention target, route, timing, procedure class, endpoint, and evidence directness, because the corpus spans systemic drugs, remote ischemic conditioning, local delivery, immune-cell modulation, and co-therapy.
consensus_drafts.source_agreement_label: high on method, mixed on biology
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Module 7 host-conditioning synthesis requires an explicit ontology for intervention target, route, timing, procedure class, endpoint, and evidence directness, because the corpus spans systemic drugs, remote ischemic conditioning, local delivery, immune-cell modulation, and co-therapy.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Cross-field host-conditioning evidence is hypothesis-generating for SCI reconstruction and should remain confidence-stratified until full-text extraction confirms dose, route, timing, host target, and remodeling endpoint.
consensus_drafts.source_agreement_label: high on method, mixed on biology
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Cross-field host-conditioning evidence is hypothesis-generating for SCI reconstruction and should remain confidence-stratified until full-text extraction confirms dose, route, timing, host target, and remodeling endpoint.
